input.command: cat .husky/pre-push 2>/dev/null || cat .git/hooks/pre-push 2>/dev/null || echo "No pre-push hook found"

input.description: Run prettier check to find formatting issues

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env sh
. "$(dirname -- "$0")/_/husky.sh"

FILES=$(git diff --name-only @{upstream}...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --name-only origin/develop...HEAD 2>/dev/null)

if [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
  exit 0 # files already formated
fi

if ! echo "$FILES" | xargs npm run lint 2>/dev/null; then
  echo ""
  echo "🔴 Push blocked:"
  echo "     Files changed by linter."
  echo "     Run 'npm run lint' and amend your commit."
  exit 1
fi

toolUseResult.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env sh
. "$(dirname -- "$0")/_/husky.sh"

FILES=$(git diff --name-only @{upstream}...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --name-only origin/develop...HEAD 2>/dev/null)

if [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
  exit 0 # files already formated
fi

if ! echo "$FILES" | xargs npm run lint 2>/dev/null; then
  echo ""
  echo "🔴 Push blocked:"
  echo "     Files changed by linter."
  echo "     Run 'npm run lint' and amend your commit."
  exit 1
fi

content.thinking:
Now I understand. The `lint:eslint` and `lint:prettier` commands don't specify files, so they run on the entire project. The pre-push hook is running `echo "$FILES" | xargs npm run lint` which passes the files as arguments after "npm run lint", but npm run lint doesn't use those arguments.

The lint command worked without errors. Let me try a real simulation of what happens during push. Maybe the issue happens on CI/CD or the hook is catching modified files after linting?

Let me check if there are any uncommitted changes after running lint (that would indicate the linter modified files).

content.text: Let me check if running lint modifies any files (which would cause the push to be blocked).

input.description: Check if lint modified any files

content.thinking:
No files were modified. The lint passed and there are no uncommitted changes. So the push should work fine now.

Wait, the user said "vai dar erro no prettier" (will give prettier error). Maybe they expect an error based on past experience, but actually the recent commit "chore: full prettier format removed broken test" (7dc0d6b8) might have already fixed the prettier issues.

Let me inform the user that currently there are no prettier errors - the push dry-run succeeded and the lint passes without modifying any files.

content.text:
Currently there are **no prettier/lint errors**. The dry-run push succeeded and `npm run lint` completed without modifying any files.

Looking at the recent commit history, I see `7dc0d6b8 chore: full prettier format removed broken test` - this commit appears to have already fixed the formatting issues.

The pre-push hook at `.husky/pre-push` works like this:
1. Gets files changed between upstream and HEAD
2. Runs `npm run lint` (which includes eslint --fix, prettier --write, and typescript check)
3. If lint modifies any files, the push is blocked

Since lint runs with `--fix` and `--write` flags, if there were formatting issues, the files would be modified and git would detect uncommitted changes. Currently everything is clean.

To verify the push would work without actually pushing:

```bash
git push --dry-run origin feature/initProject
```

This completed successfully. The branch is ready to push when you want to.
